summary: Data scientist with hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying generative AI and machine learning systems across the full model lifecycle, from data ingestion and training to production deployment and risk-aware evaluation. Experienced in assessing model behavior, documenting evidence of model performance, and identifying control gaps through systematic model comparison, explainability tooling, and structured evaluation frameworks. Brings a strong foundation in applied AI governance concepts, including bias detection, model documentation, and quantitative risk analysis, well suited to ensuring GenAI solutions meet enterprise risk and control standards.

skills.ml: LLM Evaluation and Governance, Generative AI (Claude, GPT-4o), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Model Risk Assessment, SHAP and Explainable AI (XAI), DistilBERT, XGBoost, YOLO, HuggingFace, LangChain, Deepgram, Pinecone, OpenAI Embeddings, Ensemble Methods, Data Augmentation
skills.infra: Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Railway, Supabase (PostgreSQL, Row-Level Security), GCP, Vercel, Brevo SMTP

experience.title: Deep Learning Intern
bullets: Built a data augmentation pipeline using NumPy and Pandas, scaling the training dataset from a few hundred to 10,000+ images and manually re-annotating 500+ edge cases; conducted systematic evaluation on held-out data and documented accuracy improvements from 65% to 85.4%, providing clear evidence of model performance gains.

Developed a YOLO-based object detection model in Python to detect and classify industrial pipe inventory from warehouse images, improving accuracy by 30% and eliminating a fully manual counting process.
Designed normalized relational database schemas in SQL to store image metadata and inference results, enabling cross-team queries on model performance and inventory trends.
Containerized the model with Docker and deployed on Kubernetes for horizontal scaling of model inference, enabling the team to handle variable load without code changes.

projects.name: AI Interview Engine
projects.subtitle: Voice-First Candidate Screening Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Express, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, Deepgram, Supabase, Railway, Vercel
bullets: Built an automated post-interview evaluation system scoring candidates across 4 defined dimensions with advance/hold/reject output, demonstrating experience designing structured, auditable AI decision frameworks with documented evidence artifacts.

Designed a multi-turn conversational AI engine with dynamic context injection: the LLM receives each candidate's parsed resume before the session and generates targeted, role-specific follow-up questions in real time, requiring careful control over model inputs and output consistency.

Engineered a real-time voice pipeline integrating Deepgram Nova-2 (speech-to-text) and Aura (text-to-speech) with Claude Sonnet 4.6 (large language model), achieving 4-5s end-to-end latency per conversational turn and replacing human interviewers for initial candidate screening.

Built an automated document ingestion pipeline (PDF parsing, Claude Haiku, MD5 deduplication caching) and deployed full-stack on Railway, Vercel, and Supabase (PostgreSQL, Row-Level Security, Magic Link authentication) with Brevo SMTP for transactional email automation.

projects.name: CodeSyntax AI
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack RAG Developer Assistant
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, GitHub Actions
bullets: Maintained a 55-question domain evaluation set alongside 35+ pytest unit tests to measure and document model output quality, providing a repeatable, evidence-backed framework for validating LLM behavior against defined standards.

Implemented an LLM-based query intent classifier and result re-ranker by source authority, plus multi-turn conversation support via LangChain chat-history condensing, both measurably reducing off-topic responses.

Built a production retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system using GPT-4o-mini with semantic search over Python Docs, RealPython, and StackOverflow, indexed in Pinecone via OpenAI vector embeddings.

Maintained a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline that builds the Docker image and deploys to Railway (API) and Vercel (frontend) on every merge to main, ensuring consistent and auditable deployment processes.

projects.name: AuthentiText
projects.subtitle: AI-Generated Text Detection Engine
projects.stack: Python, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, GCP, Docker
bullets: Conducted systematic model comparison across four architectures (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, DistilBERT) using ROC curves, confusion matrices, and SHAP feature importance; integrated Captum token-level attribution heatmaps for explainable AI (XAI), producing the kind of documented, auditable model evidence relevant to AI governance and model risk review.

Trained a weighted ensemble model combining XGBoost (21 hand-crafted linguistic features: perplexity, burstiness, lexical density) with a fine-tuned DistilBERT transformer, achieving 99.1% F1 and 99.96% AUROC on held-out test data.
Trained on the merged HC3 corpus spanning Wikipedia, Reddit, medicine, and finance domains; deployed as a publicly accessible machine learning inference app on HuggingFace Spaces.
